headline: Manning and Texas ground game overwhelm Georgia in dominant Week 1 statement
body: Arch Manning arrived at his first college start with poise and precision, completing 21 of 35 passes for 252 yards and three touchdowns as Texas dismantled Georgia 37-14 in a lopsided affair that settled any questions about the Longhorns' readiness. Manning managed the game efficiently without forcing plays, letting his receivers and running game do the heavy lifting while Georgia's defense had no answers for the pace or tempo Texas imposed throughout.

The real story, though, was Texas's rushing attack. Daylon Gibson carved up Georgia's front seven with 128 yards on 15 carries, establishing a physical presence that forced the Bulldogs into obvious passing situations and left their defense perpetually behind the chains. Georgia managed just 58 rushing yards total—a stark illustration of the gap in line play. The Longhorns accumulated 482 total yards to Georgia's 190, a disparity that reflected fundamental dominance rather than fireworks.

Georgia's Carson Giles tried to keep the Bulldogs competitive with two touchdown passes, and Jalen Young provided a bright spot with 58 receiving yards, but the offense never generated sustained drives. Texas's defense, anchored by Lefau's two sacks, suffocated any rhythm Georgia might have found. The two Texas turnovers prevented this from becoming a complete shutout in the second half, but they barely registered in the final tally of a thoroughly one-sided affair.
turningPoint: Gibson's consistent gains in the second quarter forced Georgia into third-and-long situations repeatedly, allowing Texas to control clock and field position while the Bulldogs' defense exhausted itself chasing the game.

unsungHero.name: Daylon Gibson
unsungHero.why: His 128 rushing yards established the tone and won the battle in the trenches, yet Manning's three touchdown passes and efficiency will dominate the highlight reel and postgame narrative.
